title: Executive Order 11098—Amending the Selective Service Regulations

flags.justification: A technical amendment to an existing, comprehensive regulatory system, narrowing or clarifying specific procedural rules rather than creating new discretionary authority.
Extremely detailed procedural rules throughout (specific forms by number, specific definitions, specific timelines).

Multiple distinct technical amendments (mailing procedures, classification criteria, appeal standing, physical exam procedures) all serve one coherent regulatory system: Selective Service administration.
Exceptionally granular specificity throughout, including precise definitions of 'child,' 'physician,' 'dentist,' and 'veterinarian' and exact procedural sequences.
Sec. 2(b)(3)'s pregnancy-certification timing requirement is a narrow, technical anti-fraud safeguard, not a perverse incentive.
Standing-procedural-framework category strongly applies: the preamble cites twelve prior amending orders (10001 through 10984), directly evidencing an extensive, ongoing pattern of periodic revision.
Sec. 4(a) expands rather than narrows appeal standing, adding dependents and occupational-deferment requesters as parties who may appeal.
An extensive, structured appeal-board process with specific standing rules provides a real external check.
A routine, technical regulatory amendment within an already extensively developed framework; no novel authority claim.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.
This amendment narrows rather than broadens an existing dependency exemption (excluding physicians, dentists, and veterinarians); no new exemption architecture is introduced.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/18 = 0.0%. Extremely dense but extremely well-specified technical regulation.
